AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 8.5 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF GILMER TEXAS. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. ONE TREE FELL ON A HOUSE BUT DAMAGE TO THE HOME WAS MINOR TO MODERATE. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEEDS WERE BETWEEN 95-105 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 2 MILES LONG WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 700 YARDS WIDE.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY NORTHWEST OF UPSHUR COUNTY DETERMINED THAT THE WIND DAMAGE IN AND AROUND THE UNION HILL COMMUNITY WAS CONSISTENT WITH THAT OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS GUSTS TO NEAR 80 MPH. THE LOCAL HIGH SCHOOL HAD ROOF DAMAGE WITH SEVERAL OTHER HOMES RECEIVING DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES.

A DAMAGE SURVEY PERFORMED IN SMITH COUNTY TEXAS DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE IN THE COUNTY WAS CONSISTENT WITH THAT OF AN EF1 TORNADO. THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS LOCATED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 1253 AND COUNTY ROAD 462...JUST WEST OF COUNTY ROAD 1253. THERE WERE SEVERAL HOMES THAT HAD ROOF DAMAGE...WHILE SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. OTHER DAMAGE CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED.
TREES DOWN ACROSS THE WESTERN SIDE OF THE COUNTY.
2 TREES BLOWN DOWN ON CR 35.

A DAMAGE SURVEY PERFORMED IN SMITH COUNTY TEXAS DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE IN THE COUNTY WAS CONSISTENT WITH THAT OF AN EF1 TORNADO. THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS LOCATED ALONG AND EAST OF COUNTY ROAD 1253 AS WELL AS COUNTY ROAD 460 AND COUNTY ROAD 458...JUST EAST OF COUNTY ROAD 1253. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. OTHER DAMAGE CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY.
NUMEROUS LIMBS WERE DOWNED NORTHWEST OF JEFFERSON.

*** 2 FATAL, 8 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF NASHVILLE. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF 5 MOBILE HOMES COMPLETELY DESTROYED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED WITH DAMAGE TO 40 TO 60 STRUCTURES MOSTLY FROM FALLEN TREES. PATH WIDTH WAS 400 YARDS WIDE WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 6.71 MILES. THERE WERE 2 FATALITIES AND 8 INJURIES.
*** 2 FATAL *** 2 FATALITIES IN MOBILE HOME PARK - POSSIBLE TORNADO... ANY INJURIES CANNOT BE CONFIRMED AT THIS TIME.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS BETWEEN 70-85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 248 ALONG SCHMIDT ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED BEFORE LIFTING. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 490 YARDS...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 0.51 MILES.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 70-85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 248 JUST SOUTH OF THE LODI COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ALONG HIGHWAY 248 THROUGH THE LODI COMMUNITY SNAPPING OR UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES BEFORE CROSSING OVER INTO CASS COUNTY. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 345 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 1.24 MILES BEFORE CROSSING OVER INTO CASS COUNTY.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE MARION COUNTY TORNADO. AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 70-85 MPH CONTINUED ALONG HIGHWAY 248 BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 1780. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 345 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 1.37 MILES. THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO THROUGH MARION AND CASS COUNTIES WAS 2.66 MILES.

CONFIRMED TORNADO IN LINDALE AREA. HOMES DAMAGED.

ORIGINALLY REPORTED BY LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT. NWS SURVEY CREW CONFIRMS EF-2 TORNADO TRAVELLED NORTH THROUGH THE EAST SIDE OF LINDALE. APPOX. 50 HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE WITH 2 HOMES HAVING MAJOR DAMAGE. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED OF 110-120 MPH. PATH LENGTH OF 1.79 MILES. PATH WIDTH OF 149 YARDS.

*** 2 INJ *** NWS SURVEY CREW CONFIRMS AN EF-2 TORNADO BETWEEN WINNSBORO AND I-30 AND TRAVELLED NORTH JUST EAST OF THE HOPKINS/FRANKLIN COUNTY. 20 STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED INCLUDING 4 MOBILE HOMES THAT WERE DESTROYED AND 4 HOMES THAT SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. 2 PEOPLE INSIDE ONE OF THE MOBILE HOMES WERE INJURED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 115-125 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 5.32 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 775 YARDS.

NWS SURVEY CREW CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO SSW OF BOGATA TX. SEVERAL TREES HAD LIMBS BROKEN OR SNAPPED TRUNKS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED WHEN MOST OF THE ROOF WAS REMOVED FROM A TWO-STORY HOME.

AN EF-1 TORNADO BRIEFLY DEVELOPED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FM 411 AND COUNTY ROAD 1245. SOME TREES WERE SNAPPED AND AN OUTBUILDING WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED.

*** 3 INJ *** NWS SURVEY CREW CONFIRMS AN EF-2 TORNADO BEGAN JUST SOUTH OF US HWY 70 WEST OF VALLIANT AND TRAVELED NNE FOR OVER 7 MILES ENDING VERY CLOSE TO PINE CREEK DAM. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG THE DAMAGE PATH. 16 HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE AND FOUR MOBILE HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. TWO PERSONS WERE INJURED WHEN THEIR MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. ANOTHER PERSON WAS INJURED WHEN THEIR MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED BY THE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE NORTHEAST OF FOREMAN ARKANSAS WAS CONSISTENT WITH THAT OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED AND/OR SNAPPED. METAL ROOFING OF A MOBLE HOME WAS PEELED BACK. TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED.

AN EF-1 TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED HUNDREDS OF TREES...DAMAGED OR DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBULDINGS...AND DAMAGED ROOFS OF SEVERAL HOMES ALONG ITS PATH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.9 MILES...THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 353 YARDS...AND THE ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 95-105 MPH.

AN EF2 TORNADO BEGAN ALONG SUBURBAN ACRES ROAD...AND THEN MOVED NORTH WHERE IT DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS ALONG FM1793. TWO MOBILE HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED ALONG FM1793. SEVERAL MORE HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED ALONG BLACKJACK RD AND POPE CITY RD AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTH. THE TORNADO LIFTED ALONG FM134 JUST BEFORE REACHING THE LITTLE CYPRESS BAYOU. MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS WERE 120 MPH...THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.8 MILES...AND THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS.
